title: Agent Prompts
description: View and edit agent prompts, publish drafts, manage version history, and rollback to previous versions for safe prompt experimentation.

steps.narration: The Agent Prompts page shows all available AI agents grouped by their function. Each agent has a specific responsibility in the deck generation pipeline -- from pre-call research to compliance checking.
Notice how agents are organized by family. The pre-call family handles research and briefing preparation, while the deck-generation family manages slide selection and content assembly.
Let's drill into the Pre-Call Research Agent. This agent generates company research, value hypotheses, and discovery questions for pre-call briefings. You can see its current published prompt.
The current prompt instructs the agent to use formal, analytical language and structure output with clear sections. Let's say we want to make the tone more conversational for a new sales approach.
Click Create Draft to start editing. This creates a working copy of the current prompt without affecting the published version. Your team can continue using the live agent while you experiment.

Now let's make a meaningful change. We've updated the tone from formal to conversational -- the agent will now speak as a trusted advisor rather than an analyst. This kind of change can significantly impact how sales teams perceive briefing quality.
When you're satisfied with the draft, click Publish to make it live. The updated prompt is now active and will be used for all future pre-call briefings.
Navigate to the version history to see all published versions. Each version shows when it was published, who published it, and a summary of what changed.

You can see three versions here. Version 1 was the initial prompt, version 2 added industry-specific research guidelines, and version 3 is our conversational tone update. This audit trail makes prompt management transparent.

callout.text: Full version history with change summaries

steps.narration: If a new prompt version doesn't perform well, you can roll back to any previous version. We've just rolled back to version 2 -- the formal tone with industry-specific guidelines. The rollback is instant and creates a new version entry for traceability.
Version history lets you safely experiment with prompts knowing you can always roll back. This encourages teams to iterate on agent behavior without risk.
That's Agent Prompts. Edit prompts to fine-tune your AI agents, publish when ready, and roll back if needed. Every change is tracked and reversible.
